title: malacocarpus sellowianus
intro: Malacocarpus sellowianus, also known as Sello's Tree Fennel or Yellow Fennel, is a type of plant that belongs to the Rutaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to treat digestive issues, and its seeds are known to contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with Malacocarpus sellowianus include relief from flatulence, indigestion, and diarrhea, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels. In terms of herbal preparations, the seeds of Malacocarpus sellowianus can be dried and used as a spice or infused in hot water to make a tea, which is said to aid in digestion and alleviate symptoms of indigestion.
